_meta.what_this_is: Example input for run_local.py. Copy this file, replace every number with YOUR own point-of-care figures, then run:  python3 run_local.py --input my_data.json
_meta.important: These values are ILLUSTRATIVE placeholders, not real surveillance data. Your filled-in data stays in your fork; run_local.py makes no network calls and writes nothing outside your machine.
_meta.licensing: CC-BY-4.0 covers this schema and template. Your own figures are yours.
_meta.horizon_days_note: horizon_days must be 7, 14, or 30 (the model's validated look-ahead windows).

source_zones.note: ILLUSTRATIVE - your observed counts for this affected zone

corridor_edge_weights._help: Relative movement/transport intensity for each 'source->target' corridor. 1.0 (the default for any corridor you omit) means no information. Raise corridors you know carry more travel. This is the biggest corridor-discrimination lever after per-zone case counts. Keys without '->' (like this one) are ignored.
